statement: Major cloud providers cut H100, H200, and A100 instance costs by up to 45%, with H100 rentals falling from historical peaks near $8/hr to a much broader, lower market range.
why: Referenced industry reports show sharp downward pressure on H100, H200, and A100 rental prices.

impact.mechanism: Falling GPU rental prices signal softening rental demand and oversupply, pressuring merchant-GPU pricing power.

evidence.source: Thunder Compute — AI GPU Rental Market Trends (July 2026)
evidence.excerpt: cut costs for H100, H200, and A100 instances by up to 45%

evidence.source: Thunder Compute — AI GPU Rental Market Trends (July 2026)
evidence.excerpt: H100 rental prices have seen some of the most dramatic shifts, leaving behind historical peaks near $8/hr to a much broader market range today.

dispersion: conflicting same-key reports: magnitude 1 -> 2; sources: Thunder Compute — AI GPU Rental Market Trends (July 2026)
